hypothesis: If pre-trained single-gas neural networks are frozen and used as feature extractors whose outputs and gas fractions form node attributes in a two-node GraphSAGE model, then this architecture will predict radial Ar/O2 mixture etching rates more accurately and generalize better to withheld high-power/pressure and low-Ar-fraction test conditions than a baseline feedforward network and alternative GNNs, because the frozen single-gas features encode relevant physics and GraphSAGE captures inter-species interactions.
